manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pipe, file, bow, mouth, watch, force, shoe, lock, embassy.
Your opponent's team words are: mass, jam, switch, pole, hood, spring, limousine, engine.
Distractor words are: duck, server, london.
The kill words are: train, calf, bank, bar, soul.

If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.

We need to be careful not to relate to opponent words (mass, jam, limousine), distractors (duck, server, london), or kill words (train, calf, bank, bar, soul).

Let's think about possible clues:

- "bow" could be related to: arrow, arch, ribbon, knot, bend
- "force" could be related to: power, strength, push, energy, might
- "shoe" could be related to: foot, heel, sole, lace, boot, sandal
- "embassy" could be related to: diplomat, mission, consulate, ambassador, foreign

We need a word that connects to multiple of these.

Possible connections:
- "power": relates to force (power = force), but does it relate to bow, shoe, embassy? Not really.
- "string": relates to bow (bowstring), shoe (shoelace/string), but not force or embassy.
- "lace": relates to shoe (shoelace), bow (bow can be tied with a lace/ribbon), but not force or embassy.
- "tie": relates to bow (bow tie), shoe (shoelace is a tie), force (force can tie/bind?), embassy (embassy is tied to diplomacy). This is a stretch but "tie" could work for bow and shoe.
- "knot": relates to bow (bow knot), shoe (shoelace knot), force (force can be used to tie a knot), but not embassy.
- "diplomatic": relates to embassy, but not the others.
- "pressure": relates to force, but not the others.
- "arch": relates to bow (archer's bow, arch shape), shoe (arch of foot), but not force or embassy.
- "sole": relates to shoe, but not the others.
- "boot": relates to shoe, but not the others.
- "mission": relates to embassy (diplomatic mission), but not the others.
- "consulate": relates to embassy, but not the others.
- "push": relates to force, but not the others.
- "strength": relates to force, but not the others.
- "bend": relates to bow (to bend), force (force can bend), shoe (bend of shoe?), but not embassy.
- "flex": relates to bow (flexible bow), force (flex force), shoe (flexible shoe), but not embassy.

Wait, "flex" was used earlier and it was a clue. But we can reuse clues? The format doesn't say we can't reuse, but it's generally better to use new clues.

What about "pressure"? Force is pressure, shoe can have pressure points, bow is under pressure when drawn, embassy can be under pressure (diplomatic pressure). This is a stretch but might work.

What about "tension"? Bow has tension, force creates tension, shoe can have tension (tight laces), embassy can have tension (diplomatic tension). This could work!

"Tension" relates to:

- force: force creates tension
- shoe: shoelaces are under tension, or the shoe material can be tensioned
- embassy: diplomatic tension is a common phrase

This seems like a good clue. It relates to all four remaining words. But is it too risky? The opponent words are mass, jam, limousine. Could "tension" relate to those?
